_csp_notes: Strict CSP — rejects `unsafe-eval` in `script-src` so an XSS
escalation cannot pivot into RCE. Currently applied to the deployed
targets (hub, pay, pro). Remaining targets (docs, relayer) stay
header-stripped until they're re-enabled — when that happens,
copy the matching CSP block from hub (static, no ZK) or pay (ZK
in WebWorkers, wagmi RPC, relayer + shared-orderbook fetch).
apps/pro is a funds-handling app (orders/withdraw/claim, in-memory
EdDSA keys) that runs snarkjs in WebWorkers and talks to RPC +
relayer hosts, so it reuses the pay block (worker-src 'self' blob:,
connect-src 'self' https: wss:) rather than the static hub one.
apps/pay moved snarkjs into WebWorkers (`apps/pay/app/workers/*.worker.ts`),
so the main thread no longer needs eval. If a future change
reintroduces a main-thread snarkjs / Function() call site, this
header will flag it via a console CSP violation — fix the call
site, don't loosen the header.
connect-src is intentionally permissive (`https: wss:`) because RPC
and relayer hosts are per-deployment env vars. Tighten to an
explicit allow-list once we ship a stable per-environment config.
Each deployed target also sends defense-in-depth headers alongside
the CSP: `X-Frame-Options: DENY` (clickjacking fallback for clients
that don't honour `frame-ancestors`) and a restrictive
`Permissions-Policy` that denies powerful browser features (camera,
geolocation, payment, usb, …) we never use.

headers.value: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com; img-src 'self' data:; font-src 'self' data: https://fonts.gstatic.com; connect-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self'; object-src 'none'
accelerometer=(), camera=(), geolocation=(), gyroscope=(), magnetometer=(), microphone=(), payment=(), usb=()
